cot_prefix: Okay, let's try to figure out this problem. So, there are five fruits: apples, oranges, watermelons, pears, and mangoes. They are arranged in order of their prices, from most expensive to least expensive. The problem gives us some clues about their relative prices, and we have to determine which one is the most expensive based on those clues. First, let's list out the information given: 1. Apples are more expensive than oranges. So, A > O. 2. Oranges are more expensive than mangoes. So, O > M. 3. Apples are the third-most expensive. So, there are two fruits more expensive than apples. 4. Pears are more expensive than watermelons. So, P > W. Our goal is to figure out which of the options (A to E) is correct, i.e., which fruit is the most expensive. Let me start by trying
